summary: Backend deployment/config fix verified. The missing env vars (JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD, STUDENT_EMAIL/PASSWORD) are now populated in /app/backend/.env. Backend supervisor service is RUNNING with no startup errors; seed_users() executed successfully; auth flow works end-to-end via the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL the React app uses. All 7 backend tests pass (100%).
verified_features: Backend supervisor process RUNNING, no KeyError on ADMIN_EMAIL anymore (backend.err.log shows clean startup)
GET /api/auth/me without token -> 401 (confirms /api routing through Kubernetes ingress to port 8001)
POST /api/auth/login admin@gyanriserana.com / Admin@12345 -> 200 with user object (role=admin) + JWT token
POST /api/auth/login student@gyanriserana.com / Student@12345 -> 200 with user object (role=student) + JWT token
GET /api/auth/me with admin Bearer token -> 200, returns admin user (email=admin@gyanriserana.com, role=admin)
MongoDB users collection contains both seeded entries with correct roles
GET /api/batches (authenticated as student) -> 200 with batches list
critical_code_review_comments: Backend uses os.environ["ADMIN_EMAIL"] (KeyError-throwing access) at startup in seed_users() - this is intentional fail-fast behavior and the root cause that was correctly diagnosed; the .env-only fix is appropriate per the no-code-changes constraint.
/app/backend/server.py is large (~1947+ lines); consider modularizing routes/services in a future refactor (not blocking).
test_credentials: admin@gyanriserana.com / Admin@12345 ; student@gyanriserana.com / Student@12345 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md; seeded from backend/.env on startup)
seed_data_creation: None created by tests. Backend's own seed_users() seeds both admin and student users + batches on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend env vars in /app/backend/.env (JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, STUDENT_EMAIL, STUDENT_PASSWORD) are populated. Backend is healthy. Public URL https://github-sync-125.preview.emergentagent.com routes /api/* to FastAPI on port 8001 correctly. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_auth_deployment.py as a regression suite. For frontend, REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is set in /app/frontend/.env.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: /app/backend/.env was missing JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, STUDENT_EMAIL, STUDENT_PASSWORD (not committed to GitHub repo). On startup, server.py:1727 in seed_users() reads os.environ["ADMIN_EMAIL"] which raised KeyError -> application startup failed -> uvicorn never finished bind -> all /api/* requests through the Kubernetes ingress returned a Cloudflare origin/502-style error to the React frontend, surfacing as 'Sign In fails with a Cloudflare origin error'. REPRODUCTION: remove ADMIN_EMAIL from backend/.env, restart supervisor backend, observe KeyError in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log and login failure from the public URL. MITIGATION: Populate the 5 missing keys in /app/backend/.env (already done by main agent). Restart `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`. Verified by 7/7 passing pytest tests against the public ingress URL.
